UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile containing personal information within the VoyageFlow application. This entity does not store authentication credentials like passwords.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

phone.description: The phone number of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Flight.title: Flight
Flight.description: Represents a specific flight service available for booking through VoyageFlow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Flight entity.

airline.description: The name of the airline operating the flight.

departureAirportCode.description: The IATA code of the departure airport (e.g., 'JFK').

destinationAirportCode.description: The IATA code of the destination airport (e.g., 'LAX').

departureTime.description: The scheduled departure date and time.

arrivalTime.description: The scheduled arrival date and time.

basePrice.description: The base price of one seat on the flight.

currency.description: The currency of the flight price (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

availableSeats.description: The number of seats currently available on the flight.

description.description: A brief description of the flight or specific service offering.

Hotel.title: Hotel
Hotel.description: Represents a hotel property available for accommodation booking through VoyageFlow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Hotel entity.

address.description: The street address of the hotel.

city.description: The city where the hotel is located.

country.description: The country where the hotel is located.

rating.description: The star rating of the hotel (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

pricePerNight.description: The average price per night for a standard room.

currency.description: The currency of the hotel price (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

description.description: A detailed description of the hotel and its offerings.

amenities.description: A list of amenities offered by the hotel (e.g., 'Pool', 'WiFi', 'Gym').

mainImageUrl.description: URL to the main image of the hotel.

Activity.title: Activity
Activity.description: Represents a specific travel activity or experience available for booking through VoyageFlow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Activity entity.

name.description: The name of the activity or tour.

location.description: The primary location or meeting point for the activity.

description.description: A detailed description of the activity.

basePrice.description: The base price per person for the activity.

currency.description: The currency of the activity price (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

durationHours.description: The estimated duration of the activity in hours.

availableDates.description: A list of dates when the activity is available.

mainImageUrl.description: URL to the main image of the activity.

BookingBasket.title: BookingBasket
BookingBasket.description: Represents a temporary collection of travel services selected by a user before checkout (their 'cart'). Each user has one active booking basket.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BookingBasket entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this booking basket. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 BookingBasket)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the booking basket was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the booking basket was last updated.

BookingBasketItem.title: BookingBasketItem
BookingBasketItem.description: Represents a single travel service (e.g., a flight seat, a hotel room) added to a user's Booking Basket.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BookingBasketItem entity.

bookingBasketId.description: Reference to the BookingBasket this item belongs to. (Relationship: BookingBasket 1:N BookingBasketItem)

serviceId.description: Reference to the specific service (Flight, Hotel, or Activity) being added. (Polymorphic Relationship)

quantity.description: The number of units for the service (e.g., number of flight tickets, hotel rooms, or activity participants).

selectedStartDate.description: The selected start date for the service (e.g., hotel check-in date, activity date).

selectedEndDate.description: The selected end date for the service (e.g., hotel check-out date). This is optional for services like flights or single-day activities.

priceAtAddToCart.description: The price of the service per unit at the time it was added to the basket (snapshot).

currency.description: The currency of the price at add to cart.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the item was added to the basket.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Represents a confirmed and paid reservation for one or more travel services, generated after a successful checkout process.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Booking entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the booking. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Booking)

bookingReference.description: A unique, system-generated reference code for the booking, provided to the user for confirmation.

bookingDate.description: The date and time when the booking was confirmed and finalized.

totalAmount.description: The total amount paid for the entire booking.

currency.description: The currency of the total amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

status.description: The current status of the booking (e.g., 'Confirmed', 'Pending Payment', 'Cancelled', 'Completed').

paymentTransactionId.description: Reference ID for the external payment transaction (e.g., from Paystack or other payment gateway).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the booking record was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the booking record was last updated.

BookingItem.title: BookingItem
BookingItem.description: Represents a specific service that is part of a confirmed booking, capturing its details at the time of booking for historical accuracy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BookingItem entity.

bookingId.description: Reference to the Booking this item belongs to. (Relationship: Booking 1:N BookingItem)

serviceId.description: Reference to the original Flight, Hotel, or Activity service. This allows tracing back to the master service details. (Polymorphic Relationship)

name.description: The name of the service at the time of booking (snapshot).

description.description: A snapshot description of the service at the time of booking.

quantity.description: The number of units for the service included in this booking item (e.g., tickets, rooms, people).

startDate.description: The selected start date for the service at the time of booking.

endDate.description: The selected end date for the service at the time of booking (if applicable, e.g., for multi-day hotel stays).

pricePerUnit.description: The price of the service per unit at the time of booking.

totalItemPrice.description: The total price for this specific booking item (pricePerUnit * quantity).

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the owner (`userId` matches `request.auth.uid`). The document ID for the UserProfile is the user's Firebase Authentication UID, which serves as the 'id' field within the document. This collection adheres to Path-Based Ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (Firebase Auth UID).

definition.description: Contains publicly browsable flight information. Documents are globally readable, enabling secure 'list' operations for all users. Write access is restricted to administrators (managed via an external roles collection for DBAC).

params.name: flightId
params.description: The unique ID of the flight.

definition.description: Contains publicly browsable hotel information. Documents are globally readable, enabling secure 'list' operations for all users. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: hotelId
params.description: The unique ID of the hotel.

definition.description: Contains publicly browsable activity information. Documents are globally readable, enabling secure 'list' operations for all users. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: activityId
params.description: The unique ID of the activity.

definition.description: Stores the single active booking basket for a user. The document ID for this is typically 'bookingBasket'. Access is restricted to the owner (`userId` from path matches `request.auth.uid`), ensuring Authorization Independence. The 'userId' field within the document implicitly matches the path parameter for robust ownership verification.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (Firebase Auth UID).

definition.description: Represents an individual item within a user's booking basket. Access is strictly controlled by the parent path; only the user matching `{userId}` can access their basket items, ensuring Authorization Independence. The 'bookingBasketId' in the document will correspond to the parent user's ID for internal consistency.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (Firebase Auth UID).

params.name: bookingBasketItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the booking basket item.

definition.description: Stores a confirmed booking made by a user. Access is restricted to the owner (`userId` from path matches `request.auth.uid`), supporting Authorization Independence and secure 'list' operations (QAPs) for a user's bookings. The 'userId' field within the document explicitly denormalizes the owner's UID for rule clarity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (Firebase Auth UID).

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique ID of the booking.

definition.description: Represents a specific service within a user's confirmed booking. Access is inherited from the parent booking; only the user matching `{userId}` can access these items, ensuring Authorization Independence. The 'bookingId' in the document maintains consistency with the parent booking.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (Firebase Auth UID).

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent booking.

params.name: bookingItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the booking item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for VoyageFlow is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and adherence to QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters), as mandated by the core design principles. This approach minimizes complexity in security rules, enhances debuggability, and ensures scalability.

1.  **Authorization Independence (via Denormalization and Path-Based Ownership):**
    *   **User-Specific Data:** All user-specific and user-owned data (`UserProfile`, `BookingBasket`, `BookingBasketItem`, `Booking`, `BookingItem`) are organized under a hierarchical path `/users/{userId}/...`. This places the `userId` directly into the path wildcard. Security rules can then directly compare `request.auth.uid` with this `{userId}` wildcard (`request.auth.uid == userId`) without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. This crucial design choice eliminates expensive and insecure hierarchical authorization dependencies.
    *   For example, a `BookingBasketItem` at `/users/{userId}/bookingBasket/items/{bookingBasketItemId}` inherently carries its owner's `userId` in the path, making authorization atomic to the document itself. While `userId` (or `bookingBasketId` mirroring `userId`) may also be present in the document data for consistency or indexing, the path is the primary and most secure authorization mechanism, fully satisfying Mandate A.

2.  **QAPs (Rules Are Not Filters):**
    *   **Public Services:** `Flight`, `Hotel`, and `Activity` entities are stored in top-level collections (`/flights`, `/hotels`, `/activities`). These collections are designed for public readability, meaning any authenticated or unauthenticated user can perform `list` operations (queries) on them without hitting rule-based filtering issues. The rules will simply allow reads, and the queries will return all relevant documents.
    *   **User-Specific Data:** For collections like `/users/{userId}/bookings` or `/users/{userId}/bookingBasket/items`, `list` operations are naturally secured by the path. A user can only query the subcollections nested under their own `{userId}` path. The security rules will allow reads only if `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` in the path, ensuring that queries always return all and only the documents the requesting user is authorized to see, thus enforcing QAPs.

3.  **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
    *   Publicly browsable services (flights, hotels, activities) are entirely separate from private user data. Each top-level collection (`/flights`, `/hotels`, `/activities`) holds documents with a uniform security posture (public read, admin write). This avoids mixing different access requirements within the same collection.
    *   Similarly, all user-related data for a given user is segregated under their unique `/users/{userId}` path, preventing accidental cross-user data access and simplifying rule logic significantly.

4.  **Access Modeling:**
    *   **Path-Based Ownership:** The primary model for `UserProfile`, `BookingBasket`, `Booking`, and their nested `Items` is based on the `/users/{userId}/...` hierarchy, making authorization checks simple and explicit.
    *   **Global Roles (DBAC):** Although not represented by an explicit entity in the provided schema, administration of public service data (Flight, Hotel, Activity) will leverage a dedicated collection like `/roles_admin/{uid}`. Write access to public service collections would be granted if `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` is true.

This structure prioritizes security and maintainability, ensuring that security rules are straightforward, performant, and easy to debug, while fully supporting the application's core features.
